name: Gullinkambi
description: Gullinkambi, meaning "Golden Comb" or "Golden Rooster," is a rooster in Norse mythology. He resides in Valhalla, Odin's great hall, and his primary function is to awaken the Einherjar (the slain warriors chosen to fight alongside Odin during Ragnarok) every morning. Gullinkambi's crowing is not merely a signal of the dawn; it is a call to arms, a reminder of the warriors' sacred duty to prepare for the ultimate battle. His presence in Valhalla signifies the constant readiness for war and the importance of vigilance. The rooster's golden comb symbolizes the radiant dawn and the promise of a new day, even in the face of impending doom. He is a crucial element in the cyclical nature of Norse cosmology, representing the perpetual preparation for Ragnarok. Gullinkambi's crow acts as a sonic alarm, jolting the warriors from their slumber and reminding them of their purpose. The rooster's crowing is also said to be a sound that instills courage and determination in the hearts of the Einherjar. His existence underscores the themes of duty, honor, and the inevitable struggle against chaos that are central to Norse mythology. He is one of three roosters whose crows serve different purposes. The other two are Fjalar in Jotunheimr and an unnamed rooster in Hel. Gullinkambi's crowing alerts the gods and warriors to the beginning of the final battle, Ragnarok, ensuring they are ready to face their destinies. He is a symbol of the unwavering commitment to defend Asgard and fight against the forces of evil, even in the face of certain destruction. His golden comb shines brightly, a beacon of hope and defiance in the darkening world.
subtitle: The Golden Rooster of Valhalla
origin: Residence in Valhalla, possibly created by the gods.

keyMyths.title: Awakening the Einherjar
keyMyths.description: Gullinkambi's primary purpose is to awaken the Einherjar every morning, preparing them for their daily training in anticipation of Ragnarok. His crow is a call to arms and a reminder of their duty.

keyMyths.title: Signaling Ragnarok
keyMyths.description: Gullinkambi's crow will also signal the beginning of Ragnarok, alerting the gods and warriors to the impending doom and the need to prepare for the final battle. This is his ultimate purpose.

sources.author: Snorri Sturluson
sources.description: A primary source for Norse mythology, including mentions of Gullinkambi.
sources.text: A primary source for Norse mythology, including mentions of Gullinkambi.

sources.author: Various
sources.description: A collection of Old Norse poems that contain references to Gullinkambi and his role.
